schema_version: v0 — skill-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: An adversarial pass on a design or conclusion, fired BEFORE the claim (before /sorted): better approach? prior art? hidden assumption? real collision? do we back up? Also runs on any settled artifact on a cadence.
why: Catches a bad design while changing it is still cheap — the first of the five feedback loops (challenge-at-intent, pre-cost). It is the intuition loop at high tempo on unsettled thinking; the later background device is the same mechanism at slow tempo on settled thinking, so the question corpus accumulates yield from the first pass onward (Telos 5, measured). Distinct from /sorted's inward check (which verifies coverage AT the claim) and the advisor (adversarial WHEN stuck): one pose, three placements.
invoke: /challenge  (on the design in hand, any time before /sorted; or on a named artifact on cadence)
state: v0 — first cast 2026-07-15. A resolver-occupied nexus; the role-replacement staircase applies.

nexus.note: SKILL.md carries the live question set; this is the charter-face record of it.
nexus.routes_back: revise the design / abandon (didn't survive the pass — a CP2 win)

traces_to: Law 1 (don't re-derive the settled — prior-art question)
CP2 (a design that dies here is data)
owner: the skills charter gates writes; Akien ratifies changes (signature gate)
proof: skill node → done only when cairnmap recompiles green (derivation gate). IOU until cairnmap exists — tracked in proofs/.
provenance: converged in the founding design conversation (MAP.md 'Workflow + starting skills', 2026-07-15). Relation to the challenge GATE (the inward check /sorted fires) noted there: same pose, different placement.
